content.text:
<user_query>
I want some sort of hotkey that toggles between Cursor's agent running mode between "Run everything" and "Ask every time."And I want this to be done globally throughout all of my Cursor projects.
</user_query>

I’ll quickly verify the exact Cursor setting/command needed for this toggle and check your global user config locations so I can give you a precise, project-wide plan.

**Planning cursor settings inspection**

I found the settings file location from your Cursor skill; next I’m checking Cursor’s local config metadata to identify the exact command/setting key for this mode toggle before proposing the global hotkey plan.

**Searching user settings for commands**

I couldn’t find this mode in your user settings directly, so I’m now searching Cursor’s installed app resources for the exact command ID/keybinding target needed for a reliable global toggle.

**Searching external app resources**

I found your global Cursor user files and confirmed this must be configured in user-level keybindings, but Cursor may expose either a single toggle command or two explicit mode commands depending on version. I need one preference from you before I draft the final plan.

**Planning shortcut approach questions**

<user_query>
Do Option Shift 1 for Ask Every Time and Option Shift 2 for Run Everything.
</user_query>

Perfect — I’ll set the plan to use `Option+Shift+1` for **Ask Every Time** and `Option+Shift+2` for **Run Everything** globally, with a version-safe fallback if Cursor exposes different command IDs.

**Creating actionable keybinding plan**

<attached_files>

<code_selection path="file:///Users/HanHu/.cursor/plans/global_cursor_autorun_hotkeys_27580314.plan.md" lines="1-53">

L2:name: Global Cursor AutoRun Hotkeys
L3:overview: Add global Cursor keybindings so Option+Shift+1 sets Ask Every Time and Option+Shift+2 sets Run Everything across all projects, with command-ID verification to handle Cursor version differences.
L4:todos:
L5:  - id: discover-command-ids
L6:    content: Identify exact Cursor command IDs for Ask Every Time and Run Everything in current version
L7:    status: pending

L9:    content: Add global alt+shift+1 and alt+shift+2 mappings in user keybindings.json

L12:    content: Verify both hotkeys switch Auto Run mode and persist across projects

L18:

L22:
L23:- `Option+Shift+1` -> Ask Every Time
L24:- `Option+Shift+2` -> Run Everything
L25:

L27:

L29:

L31:- Optional verification context: `[/Users/HanHu/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/settings.json](/Users/HanHu/Library/Application%20Support/Cursor/User/settings.json)`
L32:
L33:## Implementation Plan
L34:
L35:1. Verify the exact Cursor command IDs for switching Auto Run mode (from Keyboard Shortcuts UI / command search) because IDs can vary slightly by Cursor version.
L36:2. Add two global keybinding entries in `keybindings.json` mapping:
L37:  - `alt+shift+1` -> Ask Every Time command
L38:  - `alt+shift+2` -> Run Everything command
L39:3. Ensure the bindings are user-level only (not workspace-level) so they apply globally.
L40:4. Validate behavior in a chat/composer session by toggling modes with both hotkeys.
L41:5. If Cursor lacks explicit per-mode commands in this version, bind nearest equivalent commands and document the exact resulting behavior.
L42:
L43:## Validation
L44:
L45:- Open Cursor command palette/Auto Run controls and confirm the mode changes immediately after each hotkey press.
L46:- Confirm the behavior is present when opening a different project/folder.
L47:- Check for conflicts in Keyboard Shortcuts and adjust `when` clauses only if required.
L48:
L49:## Notes
L50:
L51:- Your requested shortcuts may conflict with existing editor/navigation shortcuts; if so, we will override only the conflicting specific command and keep changes minimal.

<user_query>
Global Cursor Auto-Run Hotkeys

Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself.

To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.
</user_query>

I’m starting implementation now by identifying the exact command IDs Cursor exposes for these two Auto Run modes, then I’ll update your global user keybindings and validate for conflicts.

**Locating command IDs via code search**

I found the internal mode enum names (`ASK_EVERY_TIME`, `RUN_EVERYTHING`), so next I’m extracting the registered command IDs tied to those mode changes before writing the keybindings.

**Searching for command id patterns**

I’m blocked on local source discovery of a public command ID, so I’m checking Cursor docs for any officially supported Auto-Run command identifiers we can bind directly.

**Fetching documentation page**

I confirmed Cursor exposes no obvious public command IDs for directly setting Auto-Run mode in this build (only smoke-test-only dev commands); I’m now checking whether related internal commands like `smart-allowlist-toggle` are viable as a safe fallback before editing your keybindings.

**Investigating toggle command intent**

I can’t safely implement those exact hotkeys in Cursor **yet** because this Cursor build does not expose public command IDs for:

- “Ask Every Time”
- “Run Everything”

I verified the bundled command registry and only found a testing-only command (`workbench.action.devSetComposerModeAutoRun`) that is hard-gated behind smoke-test mode, so normal keybindings won’t trigger it.

### What this means
- There’s no reliable `keybindings.json` mapping I can add right now for:
  - `Option+Shift+1` -> Ask Every Time
  - `Option+Shift+2` -> Run Everything

### Two workable alternatives
1. **UI hotkey path (inside Cursor only):** bind a shortcut to open the Auto-Run UI quickly, then select mode manually.
2. **True one-key switching via macOS automation:** use Karabiner/Hammerspoon/AppleScript to click Cursor’s Auto-Run selector and choose mode (global across all projects/windows).

If you want, I can implement option 2 end-to-end next (including exact `Option+Shift+1/2` mappings).

**Summarizing findings and offering alternatives**

I’ll implement Option 1 by wiring global shortcuts to open Cursor’s Auto-Run controls quickly (UI path), then verify they’re in your user-level keybindings so they apply across all projects.

**Identifying commands for keybindings**

I found a better UI-path implementation: I can bind your two hotkeys to open Cursor Settings directly at Auto-Run and auto-fill the search with either “Ask Every Time” or “Run Everything,” so mode switching is one quick Enter/click away. I’m applying that globally now.

**Designing dual hotkey commands**

Implemented Option 1 globally in your user keybindings at:

- `/Users/HanHu/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/keybindings.json`

### What I added

- `Option+Shift+1` (`alt+shift+1`)
  - Opens Cursor Settings -> `Agents` tab
  - Jumps to Auto-Run section
  - Autofills search with `Ask Every Time`

- `Option+Shift+2` (`alt+shift+2`)
  - Opens Cursor Settings -> `Agents` tab
  - Jumps to Auto-Run section
  - Autofills search with `Run Everything`

This is user-level config, so it applies across all Cursor projects.

### Note

Because Cursor doesn’t expose stable public commands to directly set Auto-Run mode, this is the fastest reliable UI-path shortcut setup (not a true one-keystroke mode flip). If you want true direct switching on those exact keys, I can implement the macOS automation route next.

**Clarifying testing and todos adjustments**

<user_query>
Old hotkeys seem to do the same thing.
</user_query>

<user_query>

</user_query>
